name: Maryland
tagline: Home to Fort Meade, NSA, and U.S. Cyber Command, Maryland pairs a deep cyber and quantum identity with an aggressive, AI-forward IT modernization push — all while closing one of the largest budget gaps in a generation.

execSummary.summary: Maryland is modernizing aggressively while absorbing a historic fiscal squeeze. Governor Moore's FY2026 budget (~$67.3B) closes roughly a $3B structural deficit, so technology spend is being concentrated where it returns the most — centralization, legacy modernization, and AI-enabled service delivery. The Department of Information Technology (DoIT), led by Secretary Katie Savage, published a 2025 IT Master Plan (FY2026–2028) built on workforce, system centralization, and security, and stood up Maryland Digital Service, an AI Innovation Lab, and a high-profile Anthropic/Percepta AI partnership. The IT Investment Fund for Major IT Development Projects roughly doubled to $130M in FY2026, funding 58 projects. Maryland's defining advantage is cyber: Fort Meade, NSA, and U.S. Cyber Command anchor the nation's densest cyber ecosystem, reinforced by 2025 cyber legislation and a $1B 'Capital of Quantum' initiative with the University of Maryland. For Dell, the motion runs through DoIT statewide IT contracts and the eMaryland Marketplace Advantage (eMMA) portal, with cyber, AI, and modernization as the lead angles.
opportunities: Legacy modernization across 58 funded Major IT Development Projects ($130M ITIF) — compute, storage, and services to retire aging state systems.
Cyber demand tied to Fort Meade / NSA / U.S. Cyber Command proximity plus 2025 state and local cyber laws — secure infrastructure, zero-trust, and OT/water-system protection.
AI-enabled service delivery (AI Innovation Lab, Maryland Digital Service, Anthropic/Percepta partnership) — governed on-prem and private-cloud AI for sensitive benefits and housing workloads.
Quantum and research-computing infrastructure via the $1B 'Capital of Quantum' initiative and the University System of Maryland.
Endpoint, client, and lifecycle refresh as DoIT centralizes duplicated agency IT under a single operating model.

budget.intro: Maryland budgets on an annual fiscal cycle beginning July 1. The FY2026 operating budget totals roughly $67.3B and was built to close one of the state's largest structural deficits in two decades — an estimated ~$3B gap — without raising sales or property taxes. There is no single 'state IT budget' line; technology spend sits inside DoIT's operating budget plus the IT Investment Fund (ITIF) that capitalizes Major IT Development Projects, with additional technology dollars distributed across agencies and named initiatives like quantum and broadband.

stats.label: Major IT Development Projects
stats.note: Funded from the ITIF in FY2026

breakdown.label: BEAD broadband allocation
breakdown.note: Federal NTIA award administered by OSB

notes: Figures are annual (FY2026) unless noted; FY2026 begins July 1, 2025.
The deficit-driven climate favors modernization that cuts cost (centralization, legacy retirement) and high-ROI AI over net-new discretionary spend.
Broadband (BEAD) dollars are federal pass-through and subject to revised NTIA rules issued in 2025.

FY2027 budget signed April 8, 2026: ~$70.8B all funds (~$27.7B general fund), closing a ~$1.5B structural deficit with ~$900M in cuts and no new taxes; rainy day fund held at 8%. Sustains CyberMaryland, quantum/Deep Tech, and the new MD TRAIN AI-workforce program. Resolved in regular session — no special session required.

initiatives.name: 2025 IT Master Plan & System Centralization
initiatives.summary: DoIT's 86-page 2025 IT Master Plan (FY2026–2028) is built on three pillars: modernizing the IT workforce (AI, cyber, data skills), centralizing duplicated agency systems under clear ownership, and strengthening security. It launched Maryland Digital Service and Maryland Benefits ('One Application'). Centralization and legacy retirement create direct demand for refreshed compute, storage, and services.

initiatives.name: Cyber Ecosystem (Fort Meade / NSA / Cyber Command)
initiatives.summary: Maryland is arguably the nation's cyber capital — Fort Meade, NSA, and U.S. Cyber Command anchor an unmatched talent and mission density. 2025 laws revised the Cyber Maryland Program (SB867/HB1062), pushed zero-trust for public water/wastewater OT (SB871/HB1062), and broadened the Cybersecurity Council's charter to assess AI and quantum risk (SB294). Strong angle for secure infrastructure, OT protection, and zero-trust services.

initiatives.name: AI Enablement, Innovation Lab & Anthropic Partnership
initiatives.summary: Building on Moore's 2024 AI executive order and the AI Governance Act (SB818), Maryland's 2025 AI Enablement Strategy targets 12 critical domains. In Nov 2025 the state announced a landmark Anthropic/Percepta partnership (with the Rockefeller Foundation) to apply AI to benefits and housing, and in 2026 launched an AI Innovation Lab inside DoIT — a secure sandbox for agency AI. Opening for governed, private/on-prem AI on sensitive data.

initiatives.name: 'Capital of Quantum' Initiative
initiatives.summary: A $1B, five-year initiative with the University of Maryland to make the state the 'Capital of Quantum,' seeded with $27.5M in the FY2026 budget plus $185.4M committed to Zupnik Hall. IonQ is expanding HQ, labs, and a data center in UMD's Discovery District. Drives research-computing and data-center infrastructure demand across the University System of Maryland.

initiatives.name: Statewide Broadband (BEAD)
initiatives.summary: The Office of Statewide Broadband administers Maryland's ~$267.7M federal BEAD award, with pre-qualification and applications run through 2025. Revised 2025 NTIA rules reshaped plans and funding. Less of a direct Dell hardware play, but a backdrop for edge connectivity and the digital-equity narrative.

procurement.intro: Maryland's central technology authority is the Department of Information Technology (DoIT), which sets statewide IT policy, security, and standards and maintains statewide IT master contracts. Most state and University System procurements — including IT — are competed and posted through the eMaryland Marketplace Advantage (eMMA) portal, the state's and USM's mandatory bidding platform. DoIT statewide IT contracts (separate from but complementary to eMMA) let agencies buy from pre-competed vendor agreements.

vehicles.name: DoIT Statewide IT Master Contracts
vehicles.detail: Pre-competed IT vehicles (hardware, software, services) at doit.maryland.gov/contracts. Confirm Dell agreements and authorized resellers are current and correctly scoped.

vehicles.name: eMaryland Marketplace Advantage (eMMA)
vehicles.detail: Mandatory state/USM bidding portal where agencies post solicitations and awards. Register and monitor for IT opportunities.

vehicles.name: Cooperative & USM contracts
vehicles.detail: University System of Maryland and cooperative vehicles for higher-ed and research-computing buys.

entities.name: Department of Information Technology (DoIT)
entities.role: Central IT policy, security, statewide contracts, AI/digital service — leads the statewide technology motion.

entities.name: Department of General Services (DGS) — Office of State Procurement
entities.role: Owns eMMA and statewide procurement policy.

entities.name: University System of Maryland (USM) / UMD
entities.role: Large independent research-computing, quantum, and AI-infrastructure buyer; anchor of 'Capital of Quantum'.

entities.name: Department of Human Services / Health
entities.role: Among the largest agency IT spenders; benefits modernization and AI service-delivery pilots.

procurement.competition: Maryland is a competitive, contract-driven market with deep systems-integrator and federal-adjacent presence given the Fort Meade ecosystem. In a deficit climate, differentiation comes from modernization that lowers total cost (centralization, legacy retirement), demonstrable cyber/zero-trust credentials, and governed AI-infrastructure capability rather than price alone.

powerStructure.name: Wes Moore
powerStructure.title: Governor
powerStructure.note: Champions AI-forward government and the 'Capital of Quantum'; issued the 2024 AI executive order and the Anthropic/Percepta partnership.

powerStructure.name: Katie Savage
powerStructure.title: Secretary of Information Technology (State CIO)
powerStructure.note: Leads statewide IT since 2023; author of the 2025 IT Master Plan, Maryland Digital Service, and the AI Innovation Lab.

powerStructure.name: James Saunders
powerStructure.title: Acting State CISO, Office of Security Management
powerStructure.note: Acting State Chief Information Security Officer (pending Senate confirmation); former CISO at U.S. OPM and the Small Business Administration. Owns statewide security policy and the Cyber Coordinating Council.

powerStructure.name: Michael Boyce
powerStructure.title: Senior Advisor for Responsible AI
powerStructure.note: Appointed June 10, 2026, succeeding Nishant Shah; former Director of the AI Corps at DHS. Advises the Governor, Secretary Savage, and the AI Subcabinet on AI strategy across critical domains.

powerStructure.name: University System of Maryland / UMD leadership
powerStructure.title: Research-computing & quantum buyers
powerStructure.note: Drive 'Capital of Quantum' and large research-infrastructure procurement; 200+ quantum faculty at UMD.

news.title: Moore names Michael Boyce Senior AI Advisor (succeeding Nishant Shah)
news.impact: Former DHS AI Corps director takes the state's top AI-strategy advisory seat — continuity on the AI-forward agenda and a fresh executive relationship for governed-AI positioning.

news.title: Moore launches AI Innovation Lab inside DoIT
news.impact: Secure sandbox (six-month pilots, workforce training, security testing) for agencies to move AI from experiment to production — a path for governed, private/on-prem AI on sensitive workloads.

news.title: BPW advances Western Maryland middle-mile fiber deal; DHCD infrastructure grants released
news.impact: Exclusive state access to Western Maryland fiber (473 unserved/underserved premises, 48 anchor institutions); 43,000+ homes connected since 2023 — backdrop for edge/connectivity demand.

news.title: Gov. Moore signs balanced ~$70.8B FY2027 budget (no new taxes)
news.impact: Signed Apr 8; closes a ~$1.5B structural deficit with the rainy day fund held at 8%. Sustains CyberMaryland, quantum/Deep Tech, and the MD TRAIN AI-workforce program — tech spend stays focused on cost-cutting modernization and high-ROI AI.

news.title: Maryland secures $500M+ in quantum investment; IonQ HQ funded
news.impact: 'Capital of Quantum' momentum — IonQ building a 100,000 sq ft UMD Discovery District HQ (doubling to 250+ staff); drives research-computing and data-center infrastructure demand.

news.title: Maryland announces Anthropic/Percepta AI partnership for service delivery
news.impact: Multi-agency AI push for benefits and housing (SNAP, Medicaid, WIC) signals demand for governed AI infrastructure.

news.title: DoIT releases 2025 IT Master Plan (FY2026–2028)
news.impact: Three pillars — workforce, centralization, security — frame modernization and refresh demand.

dellActions: Lead with cyber: leverage the Fort Meade / NSA / Cyber Command ecosystem and 2025 cyber laws to position secure infrastructure, zero-trust, and OT/water-system protection for state and local entities.
Tie Dell offerings to the 2025 IT Master Plan's centralization and legacy-modernization agenda — frame compute/storage/services around lower total cost given the deficit climate.
Position governed, private/on-prem AI for the AI Innovation Lab, Maryland Digital Service, and the Anthropic/Percepta workloads handling sensitive benefits and housing data.
Engage the University System of Maryland and the 'Capital of Quantum' program as research-computing and data-center infrastructure buyers.
Confirm Dell's DoIT statewide IT contract coverage and authorized resellers are current, and register/monitor eMMA so opportunities are captured early.
